Elements.Text: Activity diagram describes the flow of control in a system. It consists of activities and links. The flow can be sequential, concurrent, or branched.
Activities are nothing but the functions of a system. Numbers of activity diagrams are prepared to capture the entire flow in a system.
Activity diagrams are used to visualize the flow of controls in a system. This is prepared to have an idea of how the system will work when executed.

graph TD     A[Guard] --> B[Fork]     A --> C[Join]     C --> D[Merge]     T --> E[Time Event]     C --> F[Control Flow]     C --> G[Swimlane]     C --> H[Action or Activity State]     C --> I[Initial State]     F --> J[Final State]     J --> K[Decision node]     K --> L[Swimlane]     L --> M[Activity State]     L --> N[Receive Order]     L --> O[Send Invoice]     L --> P[[rush order]     P --> Q[[else]]      A --> R[Arrange Overnight Delivery]     R --> S[Arrange Regular Delivery]     S --> T[Receive Payment]
